summary: Introduction to presentation software concepts, creating and managing slides, inserting objects, manipulating slides, running slide shows, applying transitions, aesthetics, and printing handouts.

topics.topic: Creating a Presentation Using a Template
topics.notes: Using pre-designed templates in LibreOffice Impress or similar tools to quickly create a professional-looking presentation with consistent layouts, colors, and fonts.
Starting a new presentation from scratch with an empty slide, allowing full customization of layout, design, and content.

topics.topic: Inserting and Editing Text on Slides
topics.notes: Adding text to slides by clicking text placeholders or inserting text boxes, and editing the content with formatting options such as font, size, color, and alignment.

topics.topic: Inserting and Deleting Slides in a Presentation
topics.notes: Adding new slides at any position in the presentation and removing unwanted slides using the slide panel or the Insert and Delete menu options.
Saving a presentation file using Ctrl+S in native format (.odp or .pptx), or exporting it as PDF or other formats for sharing and distribution.
Adding a table to a slide to display structured data in rows and columns, with options to format borders, shading, and cell content.
Inserting clip art images or pictures from the built-in gallery or external sources to enhance slide visual appeal.
Embedding other objects into slides such as charts, audio clips, video files, OLE objects, and hyperlinks to enrich presentation content.
Adjusting the size of images, shapes, or other objects on a slide by dragging handles or setting precise dimensions, with aspect ratio lock to maintain proportions.
The Master Slide defines the common layout, background, fonts, and design elements shared across all slides in a presentation, ensuring a consistent look and feel.

topics.topic: Choosing a Set Up for Presentation
topics.notes: Configuring presentation settings such as slide size, display mode (full screen, window), and presenter view options before running a slide show.
Starting a presentation in full-screen slide show mode from the beginning (F5) or from the current slide (Shift+F5), advancing slides manually or automatically.
Applying visual transition effects (such as fade, wipe, or fly-in) between slides and setting the duration each slide is displayed before advancing automatically.
Setting up a presentation to run automatically with predefined timings and transitions without manual intervention, useful for kiosks and displays.
Improving slide text appearance using animations, font formatting, text effects, shadows, and consistent styling to make content more engaging.

topics.topic: Working with Color and Line Style
topics.notes: Applying colors to shapes, backgrounds, text, and borders, and customizing line styles (solid, dashed, dotted) and thickness for drawing objects on slides.
Inserting audio and video files into slides to create multimedia presentations, with options to control playback automatically or on click.
Inserting header and footer text (such as date, slide number, or custom text) on slides and handouts, and adding speaker notes in the notes panel for presenter reference.
Printing a presentation in different formats: full-page slides, handouts (multiple slides per page), notes pages, or outline view, with options for color or grayscale printing.
